feedback.text: Hello, I'm a 39 year old Norwegian citizen and I am deeply concerned by the lack of protections I have when I make a purchase of a video game or other software. I grew up with video games and when I bought them, I owned them. But at some point they started selling licenses instead, but still called it a purchase. They can now just shut down servers or just disable my access to the games I purchased when they feel like its no longer profitable or they have a sequel that they want me to purchase. An example: Overwatch, made by Activision Blizzard. I purchased that game in 2017 for 20 euros and is no longer playable as of 2022, and I did not get a refund. The game only lasted 5 years, and instead they released Overwatch 2 as a "free to play" game with more aggressive micro transactions. This does not seem fair to me. If they wanted to "Sunset" the game and focus on something new, I can understand that. But I paid for this, why are they allowed to disable my access just like that. Why isn't there a law that requires them to have a plan for continued access for existing customers? There are many ways in my mind to "Sunset" a game that is acceptable. They could let us host our own servers, even just for local LAN servers like in the old days of computer gaming. Make an offline mode to at least let us play the singleplayer. Please consider looking into the "Stop Killing Games" movement. The greed of giant corporations are killing the industry, creativity and my hobby. This is the one thing I really care about. Gaming is my one hobby and my escape from daily life. Please consider saving my hobby.
